content.text: Intel is tracked per map node (see Missions & the Spire Map), in four states. It converts directly into combat effectiveness: a contractor who knows the target wears Raid Mail leaves the knife at home; one who knows it's a Bloomtusk brings Penetration. Loadout is a real decision only when the squad knows what it's walking into.

rows: The node's type — its glyph (see Missions & the Spire Map) — and nothing else.
The full node card: enemy count, body taxonomy + armor profile, terrain, exits.
Scouted, plus one prepared edge: a pre-placed position, an ambush round, or a painted target (squad +1 accuracy against it, first fight).

content.text: Intel comes from four places. Three of them cost something.

1. The corp briefing — free, flavored, fallible. Employers reveal what their bureaucracy knows, and bureaucracies have personalities. The briefing pre-sets node states before the squad spends anything; how to build the three variants lives on Corp Briefings.

rows: Tags every engagement node; misses hazards.
Elegant, complete, and three weeks stale.

content.text: 2. Bought intel (pre-mission). Credits to brokers: Tag a node remotely, or have one Scouted at premium prices. Quality scales with provider tier. Streetwise features discount it (see The Twelve Skills); the profession that sells it is the Fixer (see Fixer).

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: No intel price table is printed — “premium prices” and “quality scales with provider tier” are the only pricing language in the Phase 7 docs. The numbers land with the economy pages.

3. Legwork (downtime/prep). A Streetwise or Awareness Check vs the mission's intel DC. Full: Scout a node. Partial: Tag two nodes — and a broker hears you asking. Fail: the node is Tagged with wrong information.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: The docs reference “the mission's intel DC” but never print how to set it. The DC ladder lives on The Two Rolls.

4. In-mission scouting. From any node, scout an adjacent node — a Stealth or Awareness Check. Full: Scouted. Partial: Tagged, plus a cost — time ticks, supplies, or they know someone's near. Fail: the squad walks in blind, or worse.

In combat, the fallback faucet is the Analyze action — its cost and roll live on the action table on Turn, AP & Reactions. On a success, hand over the profile card's body line: taxonomy and armor profile. The scout calling “Shell, Value five, switch off the knives” is the system teaching itself.
Skills own the baseline — everyone can scout. The verbs live under Awareness and Stealth, and tier features sharpen them (Field Read, Forward Ghost, and their siblings — see The Twelve Skills).
There is no scouting tree. Recon optics live as drone marks under Systems; routes and maps are Guide work (see Guide); the baselines stayed with the skills.
“The briefing was wrong” is the drama engine, formalized. When intel and the site deviate, the deviation is the content — run it straight.

Sentinel briefing packets are compiled by the Threat Assessment Division and are accurate to the best of the Division's knowledge. The Division has not been to the site. The Division does not go to sites.
